concept: Err / To Take the Wrong Step / A Mistake, Named Plainly
description: To act wrongly, to make an error: a wrong turn taken and walked before anyone notices. The event noun, a mistake, comes by the ordinary rule, so the language never needs a separate word for the error and the erring.
sound_symbolism: 'Ka' opens with a stop, the wrong turn itself. 'Ne' is a small internal nasal, the moment of not-quite-noticing. 'Lu' flows onward, the mistake continued a while before it is caught.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an intransitive verb: 'mia kanelu' (I err / I make a mistake). Past: 'mia to kanelu' (I made a mistake). The event noun by rule: 'mia kanelu ki halemu' (I remember my mistake).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Naming error plainly, without shame folded into the word itself, matches a practice that treats mistakes as ordinary and correctable rather than as verdicts on a person.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A speaker who can name their own error exactly, not vaguely, is practicing the same honesty the evidentiality system already asks for.

semantic_domains.cognition: The wrong step, named without shame
